# billing

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> TypeSpec schema-only package defining the v1 billing API surface — billing profiles, customer overrides, and invoice lifecycle endpoints/actions. The parent owns profiles/overrides/invoice-endpoints (.tsp) while the invoices/ child owns the invoice entity field shape; all files compile into api/openapi.yaml via make gen-api and carry no runtime code.

## Patterns

**@friendlyName on every model, union, enum, interface** — Every public declaration carries @friendlyName so the generator produces stable Go type names; missing it yields unstable autogenerated names that break SDK contracts. (`@friendlyName("BillingProfile") model BillingProfile { ... }`)
**@visibility lifecycle decorators on every field** — Fields are annotated Lifecycle.Read/Create/Update; an un-annotated field defaults to all lifecycles and leaks read-only/internal state into create/update payloads. (`@visibility(Lifecycle.Read) supplier: BillingParty;`)
**@operationId on every interface operation** — Each operation carries @operationId to produce stable Go function names in the generated stub. (`@get @operationId("getBillingProfile") get(@path id: ULID): BillingProfile | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;`)
**Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic types** — Polymorphic types (BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignment, VoidInvoiceLineAction) use @discriminated(#{ discriminatorPropertyName: "type", envelope: "none" }); a new arm needs both the union arm AND a model with a matching type literal field. (`@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union VoidInvoiceLineAction { discard: ..., pending: ... }`)
**Spread (...) / OmitProperties / ResourceCreateModel for composition, not extends** — Models compose shared fields via spread, OmitProperties<T,...>, and TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel/ResourceReplaceModel — using extends changes generated SDK property semantics. (`model BillingProfileCreate is TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel<BillingProfile> { ... }`)
**namespace OpenMeter + main.tsp as sole entry point** — All declarations live in namespace OpenMeter; every new .tsp file must be imported in main.tsp (which also imports ./invoices/main.tsp) or it compiles in isolation and produces no output. (`import "./profile.tsp"; import "./customeroverride.tsp"; import "./invoices.tsp"; import "./invoices/main.tsp";`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `main.tsp` | Package entry point importing all sibling and the invoices/ child .tsp files; declares namespace usings. | Adding a .tsp file without a matching import here silently produces no output. |
| `profile.tsp` | BillingProfile, BillingProfileCreate/ReplaceUpdate, BillingWorkflow and its sub-settings, app references, and the /api/v1/billing/profiles CRUD interface. | BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> — adding an uncontrolled-visibility field leaks read-only fields into the update payload. |
| `customeroverride.tsp` | Customer override CRUD at /api/v1/billing/customers/{customerId} and BillingProfileCustomerOverrideWithDetails merging override + base profile. | App fields on the workflow override are @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — they must never appear in create/update inputs. |
| `invoices.tsp` | Invoice endpoints across InvoicesEndpoints/InvoiceEndpoints/CustomerInvoiceEndpoints plus action input/output models (void, approve, advance, retry, snapshot-quantities, simulate). | VoidInvoiceLineAction is a discriminated union (envelope:none) — new void actions need both a union arm and a model with a matching type literal. |
| `invoices/main.tsp` | Child entry importing the invoice entity sub-models (invoice.tsp, discounts.tsp, party.tsp, pay.tsp, tax.tsp, docref.tsp). | Invoice entity fields live in the invoices/ child, not in the parent invoices.tsp (which only defines endpoints + action models). |

## Anti-Patterns

- Adding runtime or business logic — this folder is schema-only; logic belongs in openmeter/billing/
- Editing generated files (api/openapi.yaml, api/api.gen.go) instead of the .tsp source
- Omitting @friendlyName on new models, unions, or enums — yields unstable generated Go type names
- Adding fields without @visibility decorators — they default to all lifecycles and leak internal state on create/update
- Using extends for composition instead of spread/OmitProperties/ResourceCreateModel — changes generated property semantics

## Decisions

- **Visibility-controlled single models instead of separate request/response types** — @visibility lets the OpenAPI generator derive correct create/read/update schemas from one model, avoiding parallel type hierarchies.
- **Discriminated unions with envelope:none for workflow alignment and void-action types** — envelope:none inlines the discriminator, matching the Go domain types in openmeter/billing/ which use a Type string field rather than a wrapper object.
- **BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> with an explicit workflow override** — ResourceReplaceModel auto-derives the update payload from read-model visibility; the explicit workflow field allows distinct update semantics without duplicating the BillingProfile tree.

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
